eu.entsoe.transparency.ByDomain.mqtt.description: MQTT/5.0 transport variants for eu.entsoe.transparency.ByDomain ENTSO-E events under energy/eu/entsoe/transparency. Topics preserve the Kafka/CloudEvents identity tuple and use QoS 1 without retained state because the poller emits point events from rolling time windows.

eu.entsoe.transparency.ByDomain.mqtt.Day.name: DayAheadPrices
eu.entsoe.transparency.ByDomain.mqtt.Day.description: MQTT/UNS binary CloudEvents variant for DayAheadPrices. ENTSO-E poll cycles emit individual market time-series points; retain is false so a retained slot never misrepresents an old forecast or historical MTU point as current state.

eu.entsoe.transparency.ByDomain.mqtt.Act.name: ActualTotalLoad
eu.entsoe.transparency.ByDomain.mqtt.Act.description: MQTT/UNS binary CloudEvents variant for ActualTotalLoad. ENTSO-E poll cycles emit individual market time-series points; retain is false so a retained slot never misrepresents an old forecast or historical MTU point as current state.

eu.entsoe.transparency.ByDomain.amqp.description: AMQP 1.0 transport variants for eu.entsoe.transparency.ByDomain ENTSO-E events. Messages use a single `entsoe` address and expose routing axes as application properties; CloudEvent subject and x-opt-partition-key preserve the Kafka identity tuple.

eu.entsoe.transparency.ByDomain.amqp.Day.name: DayAheadPrices
eu.entsoe.transparency.ByDomain.amqp.Day.description: AMQP 1.0 binary CloudEvents variant for DayAheadPrices, sent to the `entsoe` AMQP address. The CloudEvent subject and Azure x-opt-partition-key mirror the Kafka key template {inDomain}.

subject.description: AMQP message subject — stable ENTSO-E identity tuple matching the CloudEvent subject and Kafka key.

inDomain.description: ENTSO-E routing axis `inDomain` copied from the payload for AMQP broker filters without decoding JSON.

eventType.description: Short ENTSO-E event family name for AMQP broker filters; mirrors the CloudEvents type suffix.

x-opt-partition-key.description: Azure Service Bus / Event Hubs partition key; broker-specific annotation that mirrors the CloudEvent subject and Kafka key to preserve per-identity ordering.
